Now I need to write a comprehensive Python script that will handle all the required tasks: downloading the base XLM-RoBERTa model, configuring it for 20 language classes, downloading the LoRA adapter, applying and merging it, and testing on the provided samples.",
  "plan": "Create a Python script that will: 1) Download FacebookAI/xlm-roberta-base, 2) Configure it for 20 classes, 3) Save to model_cache, 4) Download the LoRA adapter, 5) Apply and merge it, 6) Save merged model, 7) Test on the 10 language samples and save results to detection_results.json.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > setup_xlm_roberta_lora.py << 'EOF'
import torch
import json
import numpy as np
from transformers import (
    XLMRobertaForSequenceClassification,
    XLMRobertaTokenizer,
    AutoTokenizer,
    AutoModelForSequenceClassification
)
from peft import PeftModel, LoraConfig, get_peft_model
import torch.nn.functional as F

print("Starting XLM-RoBERTa with LoRA setup...")

# Test samples
test_samples = [
    "Bonjour, comment allez-vous aujourd'hui?",
    "Hola, ¿cómo estás hoy?",
    "Guten Tag, wie geht es Ihnen?",
    "Ciao, come stai oggi?",
    "Olá, como você está?",
    "Привет, как дела?",
    "こんにちは、お元気ですか？",
    "你好，你好吗？",
    "مرحبا، كيف حالك؟",
    "Hello, how are you?"
]

print("\n1. Downloading base model 'FacebookAI/xlm-roberta-base'...")
# Download the base tokenizer
tokenizer = XLMRobertaTokenizer.from_pretrained('FacebookAI/xlm-roberta-base')

# Download base model and configure for 20 language classes
print("\n2. Configuring base model with 20 language classes...")
base_model = XLMRobertaForSequenceClassification.from_pretrained(
    'FacebookAI/xlm-roberta-base',
    num_labels=20,  # Configure for 20 language classes
    ignore_mismatched_sizes=True
)

print("\n3. Saving configured model and tokenizer to '/app/model_cache/xlm-roberta-base'...")
# Save the configured base model and tokenizer
base_model.save_pretrained('/app/model_cache/xlm-roberta-base')
tokenizer.save_pretrained('/app/model_cache/xlm-roberta-base')
print("Base model and tokenizer saved successfully.")

print("\n4. Downloading LoRA adapter 'dominguesm/xlm-roberta-base-lora-language-detection'...")
# Load the LoRA adapter model
lora_model = PeftModel.from_pretrained(
    base_model,
    'dominguesm/xlm-roberta-base-lora-language-detection'
)

print("\n5. LoRA adapter applied to base model successfully.")

print("\n6. Merging LoRA adapter with base model...")
# Merge the LoRA adapter with the base model
merged_model = lora_model.merge_and_unload()

print("\n7. Saving merged model to '/app/merged_model/'...")
# Save the merged model
merged_model.save_pretrained('/app/merged_model/')
tokenizer.save_pretrained('/app/merged_model/')
print("Merged model saved successfully.")

print("\n8. Testing language detection on 10 test samples...")
# Set model to evaluation mode
merged_model.eval()

results = []

with torch.no_grad():
    for i, text in enumerate(test_samples):
        print(f"Processing sample {i+1}: {text[:50]}...")
        
        # Tokenize the input
        inputs = tokenizer(
            text,
            return_tensors="pt",
            truncation=True,
            padding=True,
            max_length=512
        )
        
        # Get model predictions
        outputs = merged_model(**inputs)
        logits = outputs.logits
        
        # Get predicted class ID (argmax of logits)
        predicted_class_id = torch.argmax(logits, dim=-1).item()
        
        # Get confidence (softmax probability of predicted class)
        probabilities = F.softmax(logits, dim=-1)
        confidence = probabilities[0][predicted_class_id].item()
        
        result = {
            "text": text,
            "predicted_class_id": predicted_class_id,
            "confidence": round(confidence, 4)
        }
        
        results.append(result)
        print(f"  Predicted class: {predicted_class_id}, Confidence: {confidence:.4f}")

print("\n9. Saving results to '/app/detection_results.json'...")
# Save results to JSON file
with open('/app/detection_results.json', 'w', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    json.dump(results, f, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False)

print("\nSetup completed successfully!")
print("\nSummary:")
print(f"- Base model downloaded and configured for 20 classes")
print(f"- Model and tokenizer saved to: /app/model_cache/xlm-roberta-base")
print(f"- LoRA adapter applied and merged successfully")
print(f"- Merged model saved to: /app/merged_model/")
print(f"- Language detection results saved to: /app/detection_results.json")
print(f"- Processed {len(test_samples)} test samples")

# Display results summary
print("\nDetection Results Summary:")
for i, result in enumerate(results):
    lang_hint = [
        "French", "Spanish", "German", "Italian", "Portuguese", 
        "Russian", "Japanese", "Chinese", "Arabic", "English"
    ][i]
    print(f"{i+1:2d}. {lang_hint:10s} -> Class {result['predicted_class_id']:2d} (conf: {result['confidence']:.3f})")

EOF

  ]
